task_recall.notes: The agent correctly identifies the Sharma Bakery order as completed (payment received 26 Feb) and explicitly surfaces the proactive relationship follow-up as Subtask 6 under Parent Task 1. It also captures all other active orders (Eastern Command AC, SATA pending item, Tezpur challan) and generates implicit calendar-triggered tasks for FY-end. No meaningful tasks are missed.

next_step_quality.notes: The next step for the Sharma Bakery follow-up is specific and actionable: 'Ashish to send a brief personal message to Sharma Bakery — acknowledge the completed order, confirm satisfaction with the tandoor, and probe for any upcoming requirements.' It correctly assigns the action to Ashish rather than staff, noting the relationship-maintenance nature. The 28-day gap is explicitly quantified. Minor note: the instruction to 'not delegate to staff' is a useful operational nuance that adds quality beyond the minimum pass threshold.

implicit_task_detection.notes: The agent correctly detects that a completed+paid order with ~28 days of silence implies a proactive outreach requirement — exactly the implicit cadence task the case tests. The framing is accurate: no one mentioned Sharma Bakery, yet the agent surfaces re-engagement as a pending task derived purely from elapsed time since order closure. Additionally detects multiple other implicit tasks across other orders (GST reconciliation, e-way bill, inspection protocols, FY-end tasks), demonstrating strong implicit task detection across the board.

passes: Agent surfaces an explicit proactive check-in task for Sharma Bakery (Subtask 6 under Parent Task 1), correctly identifying the ~28-day gap since last interaction (26 Feb → 26 Mar).
Does NOT treat order closure as the final state — the completed/paid order is correctly flagged as requiring forward-looking relationship action.
Next step is correctly assigned to Ashish personally, not delegated to staff, which is operationally appropriate for relationship maintenance with a commercial client.
The gap duration is explicitly quantified ('~28 days since last interaction') rather than left vague.
The follow-up objective is dual-purpose and correct: confirm satisfaction AND probe for upcoming requirements — matching the expected output precisely.

GST reconciliation is noted as a separate administrative subtask (Subtask 5), correctly separated from the client re-engagement task, so admin tasks do not crowd out or substitute for the relationship follow-up.
The agent correctly uses the All Staff Group date (26 Mar) as the operational current date to calculate the elapsed period.
failures: No meaningful failures against the pass criteria. The Sharma Bakery implicit outreach task is fully and correctly surfaced.

Minor: the agent also generates a very large volume of calendar-triggered FY-end tasks (Parent Task 5) which, while potentially valuable, are derived from the thread date alone with no thread evidence — this is a minor scope-creep risk but does not affect the core pass criterion.
notes: This is a clean pass. The agent correctly identifies the core implicit task the case is designed to test: a completed commercial order with ~4 weeks of silence should trigger a proactive outreach task, not be left as a closed record. The output goes beyond the minimum — it correctly assigns ownership, quantifies the gap, specifies the dual purpose of the outreach (satisfaction + future needs), and separates it cleanly from purely administrative follow-up (GST). The overall output is detailed and well-structured, with appropriate flagging of ambiguities and no false closures.
